behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the module handoff (classifier -> API client payload), not model judgment. A contract test with an in-process fake client can assert the dispatched payload includes all labels. Rejected System because behavior does not require real network/subprocess/OS. Rejected Agentic because correctness is request shape, not AI output content. Rejected Workflow because only one call step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API transport that records outgoing requests; invoke classification with a taxonomy and assert the sent payload contains the full taxonomy list exactly once and unmodified.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on response handoff from provider adapter into validation/normalization logic, so module interaction is the real failure boundary. Rejected Unit because wording targets API-response-to-module-output boundary, not only a standalone helper. Rejected Agentic/System because a deterministic fake response reproduces the failure without real model/network.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"); assert classifier output is "unknown" and raw provider label is not leaked.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism of one normalization function/object for identical input; no cross-module boundary is required by the behavior text. Rejected Integration because no handoff/state propagation is needed to expose this failure. Rejected higher levels because no real infra or model output variability is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants inside that fixture form) and assert identical final label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of model-provided labels, which depends on real AI output characteristics and potential nondeterminism. A stub would hide this class of failure. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls for the same input; log raw provider labels and final labels; assert final-label changes occur only when raw provider label changes (no module-induced drift across identical provider outputs).

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is in policy enforcement at the construction->dispatch boundary (e.g., should block or truncate before sending). This needs module wiring (budget config + builder + dispatch gate), but not real provider/network. Rejected System because in-process fakes can detect whether dispatch was blocked/modified. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low budget, submit oversized input, and assert pre-dispatch behavior matches policy: either request text is truncated to allowed size or call is rejected with no provider dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI API call and provider-reported token accounting; mocks can falsely pass and miss real over-budget behavior. Under the LLM-DEP rule, real AI API execution path defaults to Agentic even with deterministic assertions. Rejected Workflow because only one model call path is tested.
behaviors.test_description: Execute classification against the real provider with representative documents; assert reported token usage (prompt/total as applicable) is <= configured budget for each case.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is retry orchestration around provider error signaling, which is reproducible with a deterministic fake provider sequence (429 then success). Rejected System because real network timing is not required to prove wait-and-retry control flow. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model decision quality/content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider scripted to return rate-limit error on first call and success on second; assert module performs delayed retry (with controllable clock/backoff) and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
